title: Project Control Manager
description:
Project Control Manager (FSRU / LNG Infrastructure Project)

Location: Singapore (Jurong / Jurong Island)
Project Type: LNG Terminal
Reporting To: Project Director
Functional Reporting: Commercial Director (Cost & Forecast Governance)

The Project Control Manager (PCM) is fully accountable for the integrity of schedule, cost, and forecast for a large-scale LNG / marine EPC project.

  • Programme status

  • Cost performance

  • Forecast at Completion

  • Time–cost risk exposure

Key Accountabilities

1. Schedule Management & Governance (Primavera P6)

  • Establish, review, and maintain the integrated EPC master schedule in Primavera P6 (L1–L5)

  • Ensure schedule logic integrity across:

    • Engineering deliverables

    • Procurement & vendor data

    • Fabrication / shipyard activities

    • Marine, offshore, and onshore interfaces

  • Identify and analyze:

    • Critical Path

    • Near-critical paths

    • Logic density and constraint risks

  • Define and enforce progress measurement rules (physical %, milestones, quantity-based)

  • Perform schedule impact analysis for:

    • Design changes

    • Delays

    • Variation events

2. Cost Control, Forecasting & Earned Value

  • Establish and manage the Control Budget, including approved variations

  • Track and control:

    • Committed cost

    • Incurred cost

    • Accruals

    • Forecast to Completion (EAC / FAC)

  • Perform earned value analysis (SPI, CPI) with professional judgement

  • Identify productivity drift, manpower inefficiencies, and indirect cost exposure early

  • Produce credible, defensible forecasts — not optimistic projections


3. Estimation & Change Impact Assessment (Critical Skill)

  • Prepare ROM / Class 4 estimates for:

    • Variations

    • Delay and disruption impacts

    • Scope growth

  • Assess time–cost relationships:

    • Prolongation costs

    • Manpower and equipment standby

    • Indirect cost escalation

  • Support commercial and claims teams with:

    • Time impact analysis (TIA)

    • Cost substantiation inputs

4. Reporting & Executive Communication

  • Develop and issue:

    • Weekly progress dashboards

    • Monthly project control reports

    • S-curves and trend analysis

  • Translate complex data into clear executive and client-facing narratives

  • Provide early warning to Project Director on:

    • Schedule slippage

    • Cost overrun risks

    • Forecast deterioration

5. Stakeholder & Interface Management (Chinese-Speaking Requirement)

  • Interface directly with:

    • Shipyards

    • Fabricators

    • Vendors

    • Overseas project control teams

  • Conduct technical schedule and progress discussions in Mandarin

  • Resolve inconsistencies between site, vendor, and internal reporting

Business-level Mandarin is required for this role.

6. Governance, Audit & Claims Readiness

  • Maintain schedule and cost records suitable for:

    • Client audits

    • Internal governance reviews

    • EOT and claims substantiation

  • Preserve baseline integrity and contemporaneous records

  • Ensure all changes are traceable, approved, and documented

Required Experience & Qualifications

Essential

  • Minimum 10–15 years experience in Project Controls on EPC / Oil & Gas / LNG / Marine projects

  • Proven ownership of:

    • Integrated schedules (P6)

    • Cost control and forecasting

  • Strong hands-on Primavera P6 capability

  • Demonstrated experience in estimation and variation impact assessment

  • Fluent Mandarin (spoken and professional working level)

  • Experience interfacing with overseas yards or fabrication facilities

Highly Desirable

  • LNG, FSRU, offshore, or marine construction experience

  • Claims or EOT support exposure

  • Experience working under tight client governance frameworks

  • Multi-country project interface experience (Asia)

Systems & Tools

  • Primavera P6 (mandatory)

  • Excel (advanced, analytical use)

  • Cost control / ERP systems (e.g. SAP, EcoSys, Prism, or equivalent)

  • Power BI or similar reporting tools (advantage)

postedCompany.description:
At Regional Energy, we don’t just fill roles—we build the teams that build the future. We are the driving force behind Southeast Asia's most ambitious and complex industrial, energy, and infrastructure projects. Our mission is to provide the elite Project Management Office (PMO) leadership that guarantees project certainty and success, from conception to completion.


‣We specialize in deploying world-class project professionals—the expert leaders who provide the governance, control, and strategic oversight needed to navigate high-stakes environments. When a project is critical, demanding, and complex, we are the trusted partner brought in to ensure it gets delivered.

‣Our Unique Advantage: Global Reach, Local Delivery
What truly sets us apart is our strategic alliance with a global talent powerhouse. This isn't just a partnership; it's our core operational model. This integrated approach gives our team and our clients an unparalleled advantage.

‣For our employees, this means you get the best of both worlds:
The Power of a Local Leader: You'll be part of a dynamic, expert-led Singaporean company, deeply connected to the local market and its key players. We are on the ground, managing the client relationships, contracts, and local operational complexities.


‣The Strength of a Global Network: You gain direct access to a world of knowledge, international best practices, and a network of seasoned expatriate talent. This unique exposure means you’re constantly learning and growing, giving you a career trajectory that few other companies can offer.


‣Your work here will have a tangible impact. You will be at the heart of projects that are shaping Singapore's future landscape, including:


●State-of-the-art Pharmaceutical & Life Science facilities.
●Pioneering Renewable & Green Energy initiatives.
●Mission-critical Data Centers.
●Landmark Infrastructure & Transportation projects.

‣Why Join Us?
We are building more than just projects; we are building a world-class team. We are committed to creating an environment where our people can thrive.


‣Unparalleled Career Growth: Our global-local model provides a unique platform for professional development, exposing you to a level of expertise and a diversity of challenges that accelerate your career.


‣A Culture of Experts: You will be surrounded by the best in the business—a collaborative team of seasoned professionals who value precision, integrity, and mutual support. We are problem-solvers and leaders who are passionate about what we do.


‣Work That Matters: We are selective about our projects. You will contribute to high-value, high-impact initiatives that you can be proud of, building a legacy of excellence across the region.


We are looking for proactive, ambitious, and meticulous professionals who are ready to take their careers to the next level. If you're ready to be part of a team that delivers with precision, powered by a truly global network, we invite you to explore a future with Regional Energy.
postedCompany.name: REGIONAL ENERGY PTE. LTD.
